summary: This cluster focuses on the rapid quantification of neutral lipids in marine dinoflagellates, specifically *Alexandrium minutum*, *Prorocentrum micans*, *P. minimum*, and *Lingulodinium polyedrum*, using the sulpho-phospho-vanillin (SPV) colorimetric method. Key findings established a significant positive correlation between optical density and lipid content, requiring only 1–5 mL of culture, while also revealing a negative correlation between cell size and lipid content. The research was driven by applications in biofuel production and ecological understanding, with the SPV method validated as a high-throughput tool for lipid screening across these species. The geographic scope is not explicitly defined, but the work addresses general marine dinoflagellate physiology relevant to coastal and oceanic ecosystems.
